questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A uniform thin metal plate of mass $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ with dimensions is shown. The ratio of $\mathrm{x}$ and $\mathrm{y}$ coordinates of center of mass of plate in $\frac{n}{9}$. The value of $n$ is ________
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{m}_1=\sigma \times 5=10 \mathrm{Kg}$

$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{x}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{x}_2=\mathrm{m}_3 \mathrm{x}_3 \\ & 10 \mathrm{x}_1+2(1.5)=12(1.5) \Rightarrow \mathrm{x}_1=1.5 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{y}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{y}_2=\mathrm{m}_3 \mathrm{y}_3 \\ & 10 \mathrm{y}_1+2(1.5)=12 \times 1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{y}_1=0.9 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{x}_1}{\mathrm{y}_1}=\frac{1.5}{0.9}=\frac{15}{9} \\ & \mathrm{n}=15\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Correct formula for height of a satellite from earths surface is:
questions.solution: $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{GMm}}{(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})^2}=\frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{GM}}{(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})}=\mathrm{v}^2$ ....(1) $\Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h}) \omega$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h}) \frac{2 \pi}{\mathrm{T}}$ ....(2) $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{GM}}{\mathrm{R}^2}=\mathrm{g}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{GM}=\mathrm{gR}^2$ ....(3) Put value from (2) \& (3) in eq. (1) $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{gR}^2}{(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})}=(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})^2\left(\frac{2 \pi}{\mathrm{T}}\right)^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{T}^2 \mathrm{R}^2 \mathrm{~g}}{(2 \pi)^2}=(\mathrm{R}+\mathrm{h})^3 \\ & \Rightarrow\left[\frac{\mathrm{T}^2 \mathrm{R}^2 \mathrm{~g}}{(2 \pi)^2}\right]^{1 / 3}-\mathrm{R}=\mathrm{h} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In hydrogen like system the ratio of coul0mbian force and gravitational force between an electron and a proton is in the order of :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text {  } \mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{e}}=\frac{\mathrm{kQ}_1 \mathrm{Q}_2}{\mathrm{r}^2}=\frac{9 \times 10^9 \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19} \times 1.6 \times 10^{-19}}{\mathrm{r}^2} \\ & \mathrm{~F}_{\mathrm{g}}=\frac{\mathrm{Gm}_1 \mathrm{~m}_2}{\mathrm{r}^2}=\frac{6.67 \times 10^{-11} \times 9.1 \times 10^{-31} \times 1.6 \times 10^{-27}}{\mathrm{r}^2} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{e}}}{\mathrm{F}_{\mathrm{g}}} \cong 0.23 \times 10^{40} \cong 2.3 \times 10^{39} \\ & \text { Option (1) }\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A uniform rod <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>B</mi></math> of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> and Length <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> at rest on a smooth horizontal surface. An impulse of force <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is applied to end B. The time taken by the rod to turn through at right angles will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> ____.
questions.solution:
Impulse J=0.2 N s

⇒J=∫Fdt=0.2 N s

Now, angular impulse (M→) will be

Mc=∫τdt

=∫FL2dt

=L2∫Fdt=L2×J

=0.32×0.2

=0.03

Moment of inertia of the rod about the centre of mass, Icm=ML212=2×(0.3)212=0.096

Angular impulse will be equal to the change in angular momentum.

M=Icmωf-ωi

⇒0.03=0.096ωf

⇒ωf=2 rad s-1

For angular displacement, we can write θ=ωt

⇒t=θω=π2×2=π4 s.

Therefore, x=4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A spherical body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>100</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is dropped from a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> from the ground. After hitting the ground, the body rebounds to a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. The impulse of force imparted by the ground to the body is given by: (given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Using equation of motion with constant acceleration, we can write:

v1=2gh1 and v2=2gh2

Now, required impulse

I→=Δp→=p→f-p→i

Given, M=0.1 kg

Therefore,

I=Δp=0.1(2×9.8×5-(-2×9.8×10))

=0.1(72+14)≈2.39 kg m s-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Match List I with List II :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2=\frac{\mathrm{GMm}}{2 \mathrm{a}} \\ & \mathrm{PE}=-2 \mathrm{KE} \\ & \mathrm{TE}=-\mathrm{KE}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Two planets $A$ and $B$ having masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ move around the sun in circular orbits of $r_1$ and $r_2$ radii respectively. If angular momentum of $A$ is $L$ and that of $B$ is $3 \mathrm{~L}$, the ratio of time period $\left(\frac{T_A}{T_B}\right)$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \frac{\pi \mathrm{r}_1^2}{\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{A}}}=\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2 \mathrm{~m}_1} \ldots \ldots .(1) \\ & \frac{\pi \mathrm{r}_2^2}{\mathrm{~T}_{\mathrm{B}}}=\frac{3 \mathrm{~L}}{2 \mathrm{~m}_2} \ldots \ldots .(2) \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}}=3 \cdot \frac{\mathrm{m}_1}{\mathrm{~m}_2} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_2}\right)^2 \\ & \left(\frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_2}\right)^3 \Rightarrow\left(\frac{\mathrm{r}_1}{\mathrm{r}_2}\right)^2=\left(\frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}}\right)^{\frac{4}{3}} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{1}{27} \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}_2}{\mathrm{~m}_1}\right)^3=\left(\frac{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{B}}}\right)\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A satellite of $10^3 \mathrm{~kg}$ mass is revolving in circular orbit of radius $2 R$. If $\frac{10^4 R}{6} J$ energy is supplied to the satellite, it would revolve in a new circular orbit of radius
(use $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2, R=$ radius of earth)
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Total energy }=\frac{-\mathrm{GMm}}{2(2 \mathrm{R})} \\ & \text { if energy }=\frac{10^4 \mathrm{R}}{6} \text { is added then } \\ & \frac{-\mathrm{GMm}}{4 \mathrm{R}}+\frac{10^4 \mathrm{R}}{6}=\frac{-\mathrm{GMm}}{2 \mathrm{r}} \\ & \text { where } \mathrm{r} \text { is new radius of revolving and } \mathrm{g}=\frac{\mathrm{GM}}{\mathrm{R}^2} \\ & -\frac{\mathrm{mgR}}{4}+\frac{10^4 \mathrm{R}}{6}=-\frac{\mathrm{mgR}^2}{2 \mathrm{r}}\left(\mathrm{m}=10^3 \mathrm{~kg}\right) \\ & -\frac{10^3 \times 10 \times \mathrm{R}}{4}+\frac{10^4 \mathrm{R}}{6}=-\frac{10^3 \times 10 \times \mathrm{R}^2}{2 \mathrm{r}} \\ & -\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{6}=-\frac{\mathrm{R}}{2 \mathrm{r}} \\ & \mathrm{r}=6 \mathrm{R}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: At what distance above and below the surface of the earth a body will have same weight? (Take radius of earth as R)
questions.solution:
The acceleration due to gravity at a height h above the surface of the Earth is given by

gp=gR2(R+h)2   ...1

The acceleration due to gravity at a depth h below the surface of the Earth is given by

gq=g1-hR   ...2

According to the given problem,

gp=gq   ...3

Equations (1), (2) and (3) imply that

g1+hR2=g1-hR⇒1-h2R21+hR=1   ...4

Take hR=x

So, from equation (4), it follows that

x3-x+x2=0⇒x2+x-1=0⇒x=5-12⇒h=R2(5-1)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Four identical particles of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> are kept at the four corners of a square. If the gravitational force exerted on one of the masses by the other masses is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mn>32</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>G</mi><msup><mi>m</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow><msup><mi>L</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mfrac></math>, the length of the sides of the square is
questions.solution:
Resultant force, Fnet=2F+F', where

F=Gm2a2 and F'=Gm22a2

Therefore, Fnet=2Gm2a2+Gm22a2

Comparing with the expression given in the question, we get

22+132Gm2L2=Gm2a222+12

⇒a=4L

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).
Assertion (A) : The angular speed of the moon in its orbit about the earth is more than the angular speed of the earth in its orbit about the sun.
Reason (R): The moon takes less time to move around the earth than the time taken by the earth to move around the sun.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below :
options: (A) is correct but (R) is not correct
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of A.
Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of A.
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct
questions.solution:
It is known that

ω=2πT⇒ω∝1 T

As,
Tmoon ≈27 days
Tearth ≈365 days
⇒ωmoon >ωearth 

Hence, Both the assertion and the reason are correct and the reason is the correct explanation for the assertion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A wooden block, initially at rest on the ground, is pushed by a force which increases linearly with time $t$. Which of the following curve best describes acceleration of the block with time:
questions.solution: $\mathrm{F}=\mathrm{ma} \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=\frac{\mathrm{F}}{\mathrm{m}}=\frac{\mathrm{kt}}{\mathrm{m}}$ a vs $t$ will be straight line passing through origin.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Three blocks $M_1, M_2, M_3$ having masses $4 \mathrm{~kg}, 6 \mathrm{~kg}$ and $10 \mathrm{~kg}$ respectively are hanging from a smooth pully using rope 1,2 and 3 as shown in figure. The tension in the rope $1, T_1$ when they are moving upward with acceleration of $2 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ is _____$\mathrm{N}\left(\right.$ if $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ).
questions.solution:
FBD of $M_1$ :
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}_1-200=(4+6+10) \times 2 \\ & \therefore \mathrm{T}_1=240 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A light string passing over a smooth light pulley connects two blocks of masses $m_1$ and $m_2$ (where $m_2>m_1$ ). If the acceleration of the system is $\frac{g}{\sqrt{2}}$, then the ratio of the masses $\frac{m_1}{m_2}$ is:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & a=\left(\frac{M_2-M_1}{M_1+M_2}\right) g \\ & \frac{g}{\sqrt{2}}=\left(\frac{M_2-M_1}{M_1+M_2}\right) g \\ & \left(M_1+M_2\right)=\sqrt{2} M_2-\sqrt{2} M_1 \\ & \frac{M_1}{M_2}=\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}-1}{\sqrt{2}+1}\right)\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A given object takes $\mathrm{n}$ times the time to slide down $45^{\circ}$ rough inclined plane as it takes the time to slide down an identical perfectly smooth $45^{\circ}$ inclined plane. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the object and the surface of inclined plane is :
questions.solution: Case-1 : No friction $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} \sin \theta \\ & \ell=\frac{1}{2}(\mathrm{~g} \sin \theta) \mathrm{t}_1^2 \\ & \mathrm{t}_1=\sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}} \end{aligned}$ Case-2 : With friction $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} \sin \theta-\mu \mathrm{g} \cos \theta \\ & \ell=\frac{1}{2}(\mathrm{~g} \sin \theta-\mu \mathrm{g} \cos \theta) \mathrm{t}_2^2 \\ & \sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta-\mu g \cos \theta}}=\mathrm{n} \sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}} \\ & \mu=1-\frac{1}{\mathrm{n}^2} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A stone of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>900</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> is tied to a string and moved in a vertical circle of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> making <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rpm</mi></math>. The tension in the string, when the stone is at the lowest point is (if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>π</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>9</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Given that 

Given the mass m=900 g=9001000 kg=910 kg,

The radius of the circular path r=1 m

And, the angular velocity

ω=2πN60=2π(10)60=π3 rad s-1

With reference to the above diagram, the formula to calculate the tension at the lowermost point can be written as

T-mg=mrω2⇒T=mg+mrω2   ...1

From equation (1), it follows that

T=910×9.8+910×1π32=8.82+910×π29=8.82+0.98=9.8 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: In the given arrangement of a doubly inclined plane two blocks of masses M and m are placed. The blocks are connected by a light string passing over an ideal pulley as shown. The coefficient of friction between the surface of the plane and the blocks is 0.25. The value of m, for which M=10 kg will move down with an acceleration of 2 m s-2, is: (take g=10 m s-2 and tan 37°=34)
questions.solution:
For M block:

10gsin53°-μ(10g) cos53°-T=10×2

⇒T=80-15-20

⇒T=45 N

For m block:

T–mg sin 37°-μ mg cos 37°=m×2

⇒45=10 m

⇒m=4.5 kg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A train is moving with a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> on rails which are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> apart. To negotiate a curve radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>400</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>, the height by which the outer rail should be raised with respect to the inner rail is (Given, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>):
questions.solution:
For the train, moving along the curved rails, it can be written, with reference to the figure below, that

tanθ=v2Rg   ...1

Also, from the above figure, it follows that

tanθ=h1.5   ...2

Equations (1) and (2) imply that

h1.5=v2Rg⇒h=1.5×v2Rg=1.5×12 m s-12400 m×10 m s-2=0.054 m=5.4 cm

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A block of mass 5 kg is placed on a rough inclined surface as shown in the figure. If F→1 is the force required to just move the block up the inclined plane and F→2 is the force required to just prevent the block from sliding down, then the value of F→1−F→2 is: [Use g=10 m s-2]
questions.solution:
Limiting friction force is, fmax=μmg cosθ

=0.1×50×32

=2.53 N

For first case:

We can write, F1=mg sinθ+fmax

=25+2.53

For second case:

F2=mg sinθ−fmax

=25−2.53

∴F1−F2=53 N

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The angle between vector $\vec{Q}$ and the resultant of $(2 \vec{Q}+2 \vec{P})$ and $(2 \vec{Q}-2 \vec{P})$ is :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: To find the spring constant $(k)$ of a spring experimentally, a student commits $2 \%$ positive error in the measurement of time and $1 \%$ negative error in measurement of mass. The percentage error in determining value of $k$ is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}=2 \pi \sqrt{\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{k}}} \\ & \mathrm{T}^2 \propto \frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{k}} \\ & \frac{2 \Delta \mathrm{T}}{\mathrm{T}} \%=\frac{\Delta \mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{m}} \%-\frac{\Delta \mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \% \\ & \frac{\Delta \mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \%=\frac{\Delta \mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{m}} \%-\frac{2 \Delta \mathrm{T}}{\mathrm{T}} \% \\ & \frac{\Delta \mathrm{k}}{\mathrm{k}} \%=(-1) \%-2(2) \%=|-5 \%|=5 \%\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The resultant of two vectors $\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ is perpendicular to $\vec{A}$ and its magnitude is half that of $\vec{B}$. The angle between vectors $\vec{A}$ and $\vec{B}$ is ______ $\circ$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{B} \cos \theta=\frac{\mathrm{B}}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow \theta=60^{\circ}\end{aligned}$ So, angle between $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{A}} \& \overrightarrow{\mathrm{B}}$ is $90^{\circ}+60^{\circ}=150^{\circ}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List - I with List - II.

List - I (Number) List - II (Signficant figure)
(A) 1001 (I) 3
(B) 010.1 (II) 4
(C) 100.100 (III) 5
(D) 0.0010010 (IV) 6

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
questions.solution:
Rules for significant figures:

1. All non-zero numbers are significant

2. Zeros between two non-zero digits are significant

3. Leading zeros are not significant

4. Trailing zeros to the right of the decimal are significant

5. Trailing zeros in a whole number with the decimal shown are significant

6. Trailing zeros in a whole number with no decimal shown are not significant

From above rule, we get

(A)-(II), (B)-(I), (C)-(IV), (D)-(III)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A body projected vertically upwards with a certain speed from the top of a tower reaches the ground in $t_1$. If it is projected vertically downwards from the same point with the same speed, it reaches the ground in $t_2$. Time required to reach the ground, if it is dropped from the top of the tower, is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{t}_1=\frac{\mathrm{u}+\sqrt{\mathrm{u}^2+2 \mathrm{gh}}}{\mathrm{g}} \\ & \mathrm{t}_2=\frac{-\mathrm{u}+\sqrt{\mathrm{u}^2+2 \mathrm{gh}}}{\mathrm{g}} \\ & \mathrm{t}=\frac{\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gh}}}{\mathrm{g}} \\ & \mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2=\frac{\left(\mathrm{u}^2+2 \mathrm{gh}\right)-\mathrm{u}^2}{\mathrm{~g}^2}=\frac{2 \mathrm{gh}}{\mathrm{g}^2}=\mathrm{t}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{\mathrm{t}_1 \mathrm{t}_2}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: The relation between time ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math>’ and distance ‘<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>’ is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi></math> are constants. The relation between acceleration <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>a</mi></mfenced></math> and velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mi>v</mi></mfenced></math> is:
questions.solution:
Given: t=αx2+βx 

Differentiating w.r.t time, we get

dtdx=2αx+β

⇒1v=2αx+β

Differentiating w.r.t time again, we get

⇒-1v2dvdt=2αdxdt

⇒dvdt=-2αv3as v=dxdt⇒a=dvdt=-2αv3

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A particle starts from origin at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> with a velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> and moves in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></math> plane under action of a force which produces a constant acceleration of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>3</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>. If the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-coordinate of the particle at that instant is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>84</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>, then the speed of the particle at this time is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msqrt><mi>α</mi></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is _______.
questions.solution:
Given, ux=5 m s-1, ax=3 m s-2, x=84 m

The formula to calculate the velocity of the particle along x-axis is given by

vx2=ux2+2ax   ...1

From equation (1), it follows that

vx2=25+2(3)(84)⇒vx=23 m s-1

Also, the velocity of the particle along x-axis can be written as

vx=ux+axt   ...2

From equation (2), it follows that

t=23-53=6 s

Similarly, for the y- component of the velocity, it follows that

vy=0+ayt=0+2×(6)=12 m s-1

Hence, the magnitude of the velocity is given by

v2=vx2+vy2=232+122=673v=673 m s-1

Therefore, α=673.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A bullet is fired into a fixed target looses one third of its velocity after travelling <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math>. It penetrates further <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> before coming to rest. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>D</mi></math> is :
questions.solution:
When the bullet just enters the target, its equation of motion is given by

v2-u2=2aS   ...1

Equation (1) implies that

2u32=u2+2-a4×10-2⇒4u29=u2-2a4×10-2⇒5u29=2a4×10-2   …2

When the bullet stops inside the target, its equation of motion can be written as

0=2u32+2-ax   ...3

Equation (3) implies that

4u29=2ax   ...4

Dividing equation (2) by equation (4), we have

54=4×10-2x⇒x=165×10-2⇒x=3.2×10-2 m=32×10-3 m

Hence, D=32.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A small steel ball is dropped into a long cylinder containing glycerine. Which one of the following is the correct representation of the velocity time graph for the transit of the ball?
questions.solution:
Consider a small steel ball as shown below:

As shown in diagram, buoyancy force FB=F.

Applying Newton's second law, we get

mg-FB-Fv=ma

⇒ρ43πr3g-ρL43πr3g-6πηrv=mdvdt

Let 43mπR3gρ-ρL=K1 and 6πηrm=K2, then we get

⇒dvdt=K1-K2v

Integrating both sides, we get

⇒∫0vdvK1-K2v=∫0tdt

⇒-1K2lnK1-K2v0v=t

⇒lnK1-K2vK1=-K2t

⇒K1-K2v=K1e-K2t

⇒v=K1K21-e-K2t

Clearly, option B matches with the given variation of velocity with time.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Ratio of radius of gyration of a hollow sphere to that of a solid cylinder of equal mass, for moment of Inertia about their diameter axis $\mathrm{AB}$ as shown in figure is $\sqrt{8 / x}$. The value of $x$ is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}_{\text {sphere }}=\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{MR}^2=\mathrm{Mk}_1^2 \\ & \mathrm{I}_{\text {cylinder }}=\frac{1}{12} \mathrm{M}\left(4 \mathrm{R}^2\right)+\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{MR}^2+\mathrm{M}(2 \mathrm{R})^2 \\ & =\frac{67}{12} \mathrm{MR}^2=\mathrm{Mk}_2^2 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{k}_1}{\mathrm{k}_2}=\sqrt{\frac{2}{3} \cdot \frac{12}{67}}=\sqrt{\frac{8}{67}}\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If the radius of earth is reduced to three-fourth of its present value without change in its mass then value of duration of the day of earth will be ______ hours 30 minutes.
questions.solution:
By conservation of angular momentum
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{I}_1 \omega_1=\mathrm{I}_2 \omega_2 \\
& \left(\frac{2}{5} \mathrm{MR}^2\right) \frac{2 \pi}{\mathrm{T}_1}=\frac{2}{5} \mathrm{M}\left(\frac{3}{4} \mathrm{R}\right)^2 \frac{2 \pi}{\mathrm{T}_2} \\
& \frac{1}{\mathrm{~T}_1}=\frac{9}{16 \mathrm{~T}_2} \\
& \frac{1}{\mathrm{~T}_2}=\frac{9}{16} \times \mathrm{T}_1=\frac{9}{16} \times 24 \mathrm{hr}=\frac{27}{2} \mathrm{hr}=13 \mathrm{hr} 30 \mathrm{mins} .
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Three balls of masses $2 \mathrm{~kg}, 4 \mathrm{~kg}$ and $6 \mathrm{~kg}$ respectively are arranged at centre of the edges of an equilateral triangle of side $2 \mathrm{~m}$. The moment of intertia of the system about an axis through the centroid and perpendicular to the plane of triangle, will be _______ $\mathrm{kg} \mathrm{m}^2$.
questions.solution: Moment of inertia about $\mathrm{C}$ and perpendicular to the plane is : $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{I} & =\mathrm{r}^2[2+4+6] \\ & =\frac{1}{3} \times 12 \\ \mathrm{I} & =4 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2 \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A block is simply released from the top of an inclined plane as shown in the figure above. The maximum compression in the spring when the block hits the spring is :
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{w}_{\mathrm{g}}+\mathrm{w}_{\mathrm{Fr}}+\mathrm{w}_{\mathrm{s}}=\Delta \mathrm{KE} \\ & 5 \times 10 \times 5-0.5 \times 5 \times 10 \times \mathrm{x}-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Kx}^2=0-0 \\ & 250=25 \mathrm{x}+50 \mathrm{x}^2 \\ & 2 \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{x}-10=0 \\ & \mathrm{x}=2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A circular disc reaches from top to bottom of an inclined plane of length $l$. When it slips down the plane, if takes $t \mathrm{~s}$. When it rolls down the plane then it takes $\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right)^{1 / 2} t \mathrm{~s}$, where $\alpha$ is _________
questions.solution:
For slipping
$\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} \sin \theta \\
& \ell=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{at}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}}\end{aligned}$
For rolling
$\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{a}^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}{1+\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}}\left[\mathrm{k}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{2}}\right] \\& \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}^{\prime}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}{3} \\& \ell=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{a}^{\prime}\left(\mathrm{t}^{\prime}\right)^2 \\& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^{\prime}=\sqrt{\frac{6 \ell}{2 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}}=\sqrt{\frac{\alpha}{2}} \sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}} \\
& \Rightarrow \alpha=3\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Four particles, each of mass 1 kg are placed at four corners of a square of side 2 m. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through one of its vertex is ______ kg m2.
questions.solution:
The formula to calculate the moment of inertia of a system of particles about a point can be written as

I=∑i=1nmiri2   ...1

where, mi is the mass of the ith particle situated at a distance ri from the point.

With reference to the above diagram, it can be written that the net moment of inertia of the system of particles is

I=ma2+ma2+m(2a)2=4ma2=4×1 kg×2 m2=16 kg m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A cylinder is rolling down on an inclined plane of inclination <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. Its acceleration during rolling down will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _______(use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>).
questions.solution:
For rolling motion, the acceleration of the cylinder along the inclined plane can be expressed as

a=gsinθ1+IcmMR2   ...1

The moment of inertia of the cylinder can be written as

Icm=12MR2   ...2

From equations (1) and (2), it follows that

a=gsinθ1+12=2×10×323=103 m s-2

Therefore, x=10.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: Consider a disc of mass 5 kg, radius 2 m, rotating with angular velocity of 10 rad s-1 about an axis perpendicular to the plane of rotation. An identical disc is kept gently over the rotating disc along the same axis. The energy dissipated so that both the discs continue to rotate together without slipping is _________J.
questions.solution:
Initial angular momentum, Li=Iωi=MR22·ω=100 kg m2 s-1

Initial kinetic energy, Ei=12·MR22·ω2=500 J

From conservation of angular momentum, we can write

L→i=L→f⇒100=2Iωf

⇒ωf=5 rad s-1

Ef=2×12·5(2)22·(5)2=250 J

Therefore, required energy loss ΔE=250 J

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
The equation of stationary wave is :
$y=2 a \sin \left(\frac{2 \pi n t}{\lambda}\right) \cos \left(\frac{2 \pi x}{\lambda}\right) \text {. }$
Which of the following is NOT correct :
questions.solution:
Comparing the given equation with standard equation of standing $\frac{2 \pi n}{\lambda}=\omega \& \frac{2 \pi}{\lambda}=k$
$\begin{aligned}
& {\left[\frac{\mathrm{n}}{\lambda}\right]=[\omega]=\mathrm{T}^{-1}} \\
& {[\mathrm{nt}]=[\lambda]=\mathrm{L}} \\
& {[\mathrm{n}]=[\lambda \omega]=\mathrm{LT}^{-1}} \\
& {[\mathrm{x}]=[\lambda]=\mathrm{L}}
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If $\mathrm{G}$ be the gravitational constant and $\mathrm{u}$ be the energy density then which of the following quantity have the dimensions as that of the $\sqrt{\mathrm{uG}}$ :

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement (I) : Planck's constant and angular momentum have the same dimensions.

Statement (II) : Linear momentum and moment of force have the same dimensions.

In light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
questions.solution:
The dimensions of the required quantities can be written as

E=hν⇒h=Eν⇒[h]=ML2T-2T-1=ML2T-1

[L]=mvr=MLT-1L=ML2 T-1

[p]=mv=MLT-1=ML1 T-1

[τ]=Fr=MLT-2L=ML2 T-2

where, h  is Planck's constant, L is angular momentum, p is linear momentum and τ is moment of force.

From the above analysis, it can be concluded that Statement I is true, but statement II is false.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
Match List-I with List-II. 

 List-I  List-II  A.  Coefficient of viscosity  I. ML2 T-2 B.  Surface Tension  II. ML2 T-1 C.  Angular momentum  III. ML-1 T-1 D.  Rotational kinetic energy  IV. ML0 T-2
questions.solution:
Viscous force is given by, F=ηAdvdy.

Hence, MLT-2=ηL2T-1

⇒η=ML-1T-1

Surface tension is given by, ST=Fℓ.

Hence, dimension will be =MLT-2[L]=ML0T-2

Angular momentum, L=mvr=ML2T-1

Rotational kinetic energy, K.E=12Iω2=ML2T-2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q:
A body of $m \mathrm{~kg}$ slides from rest along the curve of vertical circle from point $A$ to $B$ in friction less path. The velocity of the body at $B$ is:
$\text { (given, } R=14 \mathrm{~m}, g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2 \text { and } \sqrt{2}=1.4 \text { ) }$
questions.solution: Apply W.E.T. from A to B $\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \mathrm{W}_{\mathrm{mg}}=\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{B}}-\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{A}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{mg} \times\left(\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{2}}+\mathrm{R}\right)=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{B}}^2-0\left\{\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{A}}=0 \text { rest }\right\} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{mgR} \frac{(\sqrt{2}+1)}{\sqrt{2}}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{B}}^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{\mathrm{gR} \frac{2(\sqrt{2}+1)}{\sqrt{2}}}=\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{B}} \\ & \Rightarrow \sqrt{\frac{10 \times 14 \times 2(2.4)}{1.4}}=\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{B}} \\ & \Rightarrow 21.9=\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{B}} \end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: If a rubber ball falls from a height $h$ and rebounds upto the height of $h / 2$. The percentage loss of total energy of the initial system as well as velocity ball before it strikes the ground, respectively, are :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Velocity just before collision }=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gh}} \\
& \text { Velocity just after collision }=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{~g}\left(\frac{\mathrm{h}}{2}\right)} \\
& \therefore \Delta \mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m}(2 \mathrm{gh})-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mgh} \\
& =\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mgh} \\
& \therefore \% \text { loss in energy } \\
& =\frac{\Delta \mathrm{KE}}{\mathrm{KE}_{\mathrm{i}}} \times 100=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mgh}}{\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mg} 2 \mathrm{~h}} \times 100=50 \%
\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A body is moving unidirectionally under the influence of a constant power source. Its displacement in time $\mathrm{t}$ is proportional to :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & P=\operatorname{constant} \Rightarrow \mathrm{FV}=\text { constant } \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{m} \frac{\mathrm{dV}}{\mathrm{dt}} \mathrm{V}=\text { constant } \\ & \int_0^{\mathrm{V}} \mathrm{VdV}=(\mathrm{C}) \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{dt} \\ & \left(\frac{\mathrm{V}^2}{2}\right)=\mathrm{Ct} \\ & \mathrm{V} \propto \mathrm{t}^{1 / 2} \\ & \frac{\mathrm{ds}}{\mathrm{dt}} \propto \mathrm{t}^{1 / 2} \\ & \int_0^{\mathrm{S}} \mathrm{ds}=\mathrm{K} \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{t}^{1 / 2} \mathrm{dt} \\ & \mathrm{S}=\mathrm{K} \times \frac{2}{3} \mathrm{t}^{3 / 2} \\ & \mathrm{~S} \propto \mathrm{t}^{3 / 2}\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ displacement is proportional to $(\mathrm{t})^{3 / 2}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A circular table is rotating with an angular velocity of $\omega \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$ about its axis (see figure). There is a smooth groove along a radial direction on the table. A steel ball is gently placed at a distance of $1 \mathrm{~m}$ on the groove. All the surfaces are smooth. If the radius of the table is $3 \mathrm{~m}$, the radial velocity of the ball w.r.t. the table at the time ball leaves the table is $x \sqrt{2} \omega \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$, where the value of $x$ is _____.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & a_c=\omega^2 x \\ & \frac{v d v}{d x}=\omega^2 x \\ & \int_0^v v d v=\int_1^3 \omega^2 x d x \\ & \frac{v^2}{2}=\omega^2\left[\frac{x^2}{2}\right] \\ & \frac{v^2}{2}=\frac{\omega^2}{2}\left[3^2-1^2\right] \\ & v=2 \sqrt{2} \omega \\ & x=2\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A bob of mass m is suspended by a light string of length L. It is imparted a minimum horizontal velocity at the lowest point A such that it just completes half circle reaching the top most position B. The ratio of kinetic energies ( K.E. )A( K.E. )B is :
questions.solution:
The velocity given is minimum, just enough to complete verticle circle. At the top most point, tension of the string will be zero and gravitational force will provide the required centripetal force.

Therefore, 

mg=mVH2L⇒12mVH2=12gL

Apply energy conservation between point A and B, we get

12mVL2=12mVH2+mg(2 L)

⇒VL=5gL

Also, VH=gL

Required ratio, (K.E)A(K.E)B=12 m(5gL)212 m(gL)2=51

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> begins to move under the action of a time dependent force given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mover><mi>F</mi><mo>→</mo></mover><mo>=</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>6</mn><mi>t</mi><mo> </mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn><msup><mi>t</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">j</mi><mo>^</mo></mover></mrow></mfenced><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math>. The power developed by the force at the time <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math> is given by:
questions.solution:
Given: F→=6t i^+6t2 j^ N

Now, F→=ma→=6t i^+6t2 j^

⇒a→=F→m=3t i^+3t2 j^

Hence, velocity v→=∫0ta→dt=3t22i^+t3j^

Required power, P=F→.v→=9t3+6t5 W

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Must Do Top Qs of JEE Main 2024
questions.q: A ball suspended by a thread swings in a vertical plane so that its magnitude of acceleration in the extreme position and lowest position are equal. The angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">θ</mi><mo>)</mo></math> of thread deflection in the extreme position will be :
questions.solution:
At the lowermost point, there is no tangential force on the ball. Thus, there exists no tangential acceleration at the bottommost point, only the centripetal acceleration survives.

On the other hand, in the extreme position of the ball, there is no centripetal acceleration as the ball becomes momentarily at rest at that position. Here, only tangential acceleration survives.

Thus, with respect to the figure above, equating the kinetic energy at the bottommost point to the potential energy at the extreme point, we have
12mv2=mgl(1-cosθ)⇒v2l=2g(1-cosθ)   ...1

Now, the net acceleration at at the extreme point can be written as

at=gsinθ   ...2

According to the given problem, it follows that for the two positions of the ball,

at=v2l   ...3

Equations (1), (2) and (3) imply that

gsinθ=2g(1-cosθ)⇒2sinθ2cosθ2=4sin2θ2⇒tanθ2=12⇒θ=2tan-112
